Retina Pigmented layer, Neural layer Tongue Fungiform papillae, Filiform papillae STATION FIVE: EYE DISSECTION • First, determine the external surface features and structures of the cow eye. You should be able to identify: the sclera, or the white of the eye, the cornea, which is the semi-transparent layer covering the front part of the eye, and the optic nerve which protrudes from the posterior portion of the eye. There may be periorbital fat or external muscles still attached to the eye. • Use dissecting scissors to cut away any fat or muscle attached to the eye. • Using a scalpel, cut through the sclera along the frontal plane.
